metaTitle: Text Diff Checker — Compare Two Texts & Find Differences Online
metaDescription: Compare two texts side by side and instantly highlight differences. Perfect for proofreading, code review, and document comparison. 100% private — your text never leaves your device.

howto.description: Type or paste the original version of your text into the left box.
Type or paste the new or modified version into the right box.
Differences are highlighted in real time — green for additions, red for deletions.

why: 100% private — your text never leaves your browser
Real-time diff highlighting as you type
Clear color coding — green for added, red for removed
Works completely offline after the initial page load
No sign-up, no limits, completely free forever

faq.q: What is a text diff checker?
faq.a: A text diff checker compares two versions of text and highlights what has been added, removed, or changed between them.
No. Everything runs entirely in your browser. Your text is never sent to any server or saved anywhere.

faq.q: What is the difference between word and line mode?
faq.a: Word mode highlights individual word changes within each line, while line mode treats each entire line as a single unit of comparison.

faq.q: Can I use this to compare code?
faq.a: Yes. The tool works with any plain text including source code, JSON, HTML, CSS, and more.

faq.q: Is there a text size limit?
faq.a: No. There are no limits on text size. Everything is processed locally in your browser.

technicalSpecs.useCase: Best for comparing sentences, paragraphs, and prose documents.
Best for comparing code, configs, and structured text files.

troubleshooting: If no differences appear, make sure both text boxes have content and are not identical.
For large texts, switch to Line mode for faster and cleaner results.
Whitespace-only differences may appear subtle — look for faint highlights.
proTips: Use Line mode for comparing code or JSON to get cleaner, line-by-line results.
Paste before and after versions of an email or blog post to catch accidental edits.
Use the diff result to quickly identify what changed in a contract or legal document.
